sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: AS Monaco has shown strong attacking form in their last 5 matches, scoring 12 goals, though their defense has been leaky with 6 goals conceded. Le Havre, on the other hand, struggles offensively, scoring only 1 goal in their last 5 matches while conceding 5, indicating poor form.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Performance
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, AS Monaco has been dominant with 1 win and 1 draw, scoring 4 goals against Le Havre's 2. Both teams often score in these encounters, with 3 of the last 5 meetings featuring goals from both sides.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics and Streaks
sections.text: Monaco's streak of over 2.5 goals in their last 5 home matches suggests a high-scoring game, while Le Havre's inability to win in their last 5 away games points to their vulnerability. Both teams tend to see under 4.5 cards in matches, indicating a game with moderate physicality.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Tactics
sections.text: Monaco's in-form players like Maghnes Akliouche (2 goals, 2 assists in last 5) could exploit Le Havre's defensive weaknesses. Le Havre lacks standout performers recently, which may hinder their ability to counter Monaco's attacking pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, AS Monaco leads Le Havre by a score of 1-0 at Stade Louis II. The home side has dominated much of the game, showcasing their control with a significant advantage in ball possession and territorial presence. The lone goal came from a well-executed play in the 32nd minute, though it was unfortunately an own goal by Le Havre's Gautier Lloris. Monaco has shown more intent in the final third, with numerous entries and penetrative moves into the penalty area. Le Havre, on the other hand, has struggled to create meaningful attacking opportunities, often being forced to defend deep in their own half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Looking at the flow of the game, AS Monaco has consistently held the upper hand in terms of attacking momentum. From the early minutes, they established a strong presence with frequent forward surges, maintaining pressure for extended periods. There were brief moments where Le Havre attempted to push back with spells of their own, particularly around the midway point of the half, but these were quickly neutralized by Monaco's relentless attacking waves. Towards the end of the half, Monaco once again ramped up their momentum, finishing strongly and looking likely to extend their lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team statistics
halftimeSections.text: AS Monaco's dominance is reflected in their half-time statistics, with a staggering share of possession and a high number of final third entries compared to Le Havre. They have also created more opportunities in the opposition's penalty area and won several possessions in dangerous zones. Le Havre, meanwhile, has shown resilience in duels, particularly in the air, but their limited time on the ball and fewer ventures into Monaco's final third highlight their defensive posture. Both teams have managed a shot on target each, but Monaco's overall attacking intent appears far superior.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Player performances
halftimeSections.text: For AS Monaco, players like Takumi Minamino have been active in attack, contributing with shots on target and posing a constant threat. Maghnes Akliouche and Lamine Camara have also shown creativity with key passes and interceptions, helping to maintain control in midfield. On the defensive end, Eric Dier has been solid with important clearances. For Le Havre, Issa Soumaré stands out with a shot on target and defensive contributions through tackles. Their backline, including Arouna Sangante and Gautier Lloris, has been under constant pressure, with Lloris unfortunately conceding the own goal that separates the teams.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: Monaco's approach has been to dominate possession and press high, forcing Le Havre into a defensive shell. Their ability to sustain attacks and recycle the ball in advanced areas has been key to their control of the game. Le Havre, in contrast, has adopted a more cautious strategy, focusing on winning duels and clearing danger rather than building sustained attacks. If Le Havre is to get back into this match, they will need to take more risks in the second half, potentially leaving spaces for Monaco to exploit on the counter.
